REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR LEAVE OF COURT TO AMEND X-COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT REID RANKIN, AN INDIVIDUAL VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA DBA COLDWELL BANKER RESIDENTIAL BROKERAGE

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS REID RANKIN AND VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA DBA COLDWELL BANKER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. EVEN IF THE COURT ASSUMES THAT THE PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS COMPULSORY, AMENDMENT IS PROPER. "A POLICY OF LIBERAL CONSTRUCTION OF SECTION 426.50 TO AVOID FORFEITURE OF CAUSES OF ACTION IS IMPOSED ON THE TRIAL COURT. A MOTION TO FILE A CROSS COMPLAINT AT ANY TIME DURING THE COURSE OF THE ACTION MUST BE GRANTED UNLESS BAD FAITH OF THE MOVING PARTY IS DEMONSTRATED WHERE FORFEITURE WOULD OTHERWISE RESULT. FACTORS SUCH AS OVERSIGHT, INADVERTENCE, NEGLECT, MISTAKE OR OTHER CAUSE, ARE INSUFFICIENT GROUNDS TO DENY THE MOTION UNLESS ACCOMPANIED BY BAD FAITH." SILVER ORGANIZATIONS LTD. V. FRANK, (1990) 217 CAL.APP.3D 94, 98-99. IN THIS CASE, THERE IS NO SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE OF BAD FAITH. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Petition
Off Calendar Feb-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Sonia Wong, CSR#11288.
Brian E. Soriano, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff(s) Petitioner: Dawn Taffler, parent and guardian ad litem for Minor: Aurora Taffler.
Petitioner not present in court.

After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $130,000.00
Attorney's Fees: $22,000.00 payable to Law Office of Brian E. Soriano.
Reimbursement for medical and all othe expenses paid by the petitioner or the petitioner's attorney in the total amount of $11,059.29
Medical, hospital, ambulance, nursing, and other like expenses payable directly to providers as follows: $40,000.00 to The Ralwings Company P.O. Box 2000, LaGrange, KY 40031-2000.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Continued To Oct-21-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend X-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-12-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs REID RANKIN and VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA DBA COLDWELL BANKER's Motion For Leave To Amend cross-Complaint is granted. Even if the Court assumes that the proposed cross-complaint is compulsory, amendment is proper. "A policy of liberal construction of section 426.50 to avoid forfeiture of causes of action is imposed on the trial court. A motion to file a cross complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank, (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99. In this case, there is no substantial evidence of bad faith. Order signed after hearing. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
